title: Comparative analysis of smart city scientific research trends in the USA and China

data_scope.proposal_count: not stated in abstract/data sections; reported in figures and Supplementary Tables 1-3
agencies: National Natural Science Foundation of China

core_conclusion: Smart city research in the USA and China is simultaneously converging and diverging. Using LLMs to parse NSF and NSFC funded proposals, the authors find shared scientific inquiries but divergent national trajectories shaped by funding mechanisms and national context, exposing a persistent tension between techno-centric priorities and human-oriented imperatives that risks fragmenting the field.
why_it_matters: Analyzes funded proposals (the upstream signal) rather than finished publications; makes the techno-centric vs. human-centered split measurable across two leading economies; demonstrates LLMs as a reusable science-of-science instrument; speaks directly to research funding policy.
why_nature: Cross-national systems-level comparison with generalizable findings; methodological novelty (LLMs on funding-proposal corpora) with broad reach; bridges technology and society; policy-translatable for international research strategy.
added_value_to_my_research: Geocode the proposal corpus -> sub-national map of techno- vs human-centered research priorities
Explainable-ML audit of the LLM classifier -> calibration and cross-language fairness checks
Link proposals to publications/patents/deployment -> accessibility-to-outcome conversion modeling
Decompose the human-centered pole into health/equity/digital-divide funding line items
Collaboration meta-network + graph learning -> fragmentation-risk forecasting via community detection
